name: wifi_Network_recon
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi_Network_recon`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: python scanner_ALL.py (then enter network IP at the input() prompt); ip.py and self_Mac.py run separately the same way

examples.command: python scanner_ALL.py (then enter network IP at the input() prompt); ip.py and self_Mac.py run separately the same way

algorithms: MAC resolution via getmac per discovered host
OUI vendor lookup via macvendors.com HTTP API
results assembled into pandas DataFrame / .xls export
produces: list of live host IPs on the LAN
pandas DataFrame and Excel .xls of the above
novelty: none — thin wrapper. It is not 802.11/WiFi recon at all: no monitor mode, no beacon/probe/EAPOL capture, no airodump/wash/hcxtools use. It shells out to nmap -sn for post-association LAN host discovery and adds getmac + macvendors.com OCI lookup + geocoder IP-geolocation, all of which KFIOSA can do with scapy/nmap directly. The "geolocation" is the scanner's own IP location, not per-target.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
